The gRPC server was returning errors as in-band GenerateResponse.error / EmbedResponse.error messages instead of using proper gRPC status codes via context.abort(). This caused two problems:


Wrong status codes: Scheduler abort errors (e.g., input too long → FINISH_ABORT with HTTPStatus.

sglang.title: fix(grpc): add server-side keepalive options to prevent GOAWAY
sglang.author: slin1237
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
The gRPC server uses C-core defaults which enforce a 300s minimum ping interval (grpc.http2.min_recv_ping_interval_without_data_ms). Clients sending keepalive pings more frequently (e.g. every 30s) receive GOAWAY after 2 violations during long requests where no DATA frames flow (e.g. during

vllm.title: Fix Eagle3 with Transformers modelling backend
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This import was moved in huggingface/transformers#43765 and was first released in 5.2.0.
This PR also changed the capture hooks to be registered lazily, which is a problem when we use torch.compile. This PR also eagerly registers those hooks if they're going to be used to avoid issues with Dynamo.

vllm.title: [KVConnector] Scheduler: Fix num_computed_tokens after async KV load
vllm.author: orozery
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Previously, following a successful async KV loading, the scheduler
would update the request num_computed_tokens by counting the number of
allocated blocks and multiplying by the block size,
and rounding down if necessary to request.num_tokens.
This worked as long the last external token aligned to e

vllm.title: [Bug] Fix a corner case in _process_simple_streaming_events

We could have a delta message with both reasoning and content which would result in weird events order and content being dropped.

vllm.title: [Feature] Add --distributed-timeout-seconds CLI option
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Add a --distributed-timeout-seconds CLI option that allows users to configure the timeout for torch.distributed.init_process_group. This is useful for multi-node setups where model downloads or node startup may be slow, causing PyTorch's default 600s NCCL timeout to expire prematurely.
When

vllm.title: [BugFix] Fix engine hanging after KV cache initialization failure
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix a bug where vLLM engine processes become zombies when KV cache initialization fails. Previously, if _initialize_kv_caches raised an exception, the executor was never shut down and the front-end would hang waiting for a ready message that never arrives.
This PR adds three changes:

Engine

vllm.title: [CI] Fix bge-m3 similarity reference values after *Defination* typo fix
vllm.author: AndreasKaratzas
vllm.bodyExcerpt: PR #34934 fixed the typo "Defination of BM25" to "Definition of BM25" in the bge-m3 test inputs but did not update the hardcoded similarity reference values, which were computed against the old misspelled input. Since the embedding model produces different vectors for the corrected spelling, the tes

vllm.title: perf: add __slots__ to KVCacheBlock
vllm.author: cong-or
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
What
Adds slots=True to the KVCacheBlock dataclass. One-line change.
Why
This is the most-allocated object in the KV cache path — one per
block, 100K+ in a typical deployment. Each instance currently
carries a __dict__ hash table it doesn't need. Slots eliminate
that, dropping instance size from 344

vllm.title: [Frontend][Core] Add shutdown timeout - allowing in-flight requests to finish
vllm.author: markmc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Relates to #24885 and supersedes a subset of #32420
🤖 Co-authored with Claude Code
Overview
This PR adds support for a shutdown timeout in the API server and engine core, allowing controlled handling of a SIGTERM signal - either immediate termination by default (--shutdown-timeout=0) or wait for in-

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix inner_dp_world initialization order for multi-node TP
vllm.author: zyongye
vllm.bodyExcerpt: When running multi-node tensor parallelism (nnodes_within_dp > 1), _init_message_queues() calls get_inner_dp_world_group() which requires distributed groups to be initialized. However, _init_message_queues() was being called before init_device(), which is where the distributed environment (including

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix misleading context length error messages
vllm.author: AjAnubolu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Closes #33418
Reword error messages when prompt + max_tokens exceeds context length
to show the actual token counts and suggest reducing either input or output.

vllm.title: [Security] Respect user trust_remote_code setting in NemotronVL and KimiK25
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Replace hardcoded trust_remote_code=True with the user's configured
trust_remote_code setting from model_config in both nemotron_vl.py and
kimi_k25.py. This prevents bypassing the user's explicit
--trust-remote-code=False security opt-out when loading sub-components.
GHSA-7972-pg2x-xr59
Signed-off-b

vllm.title: [BugFix] avoid infinite loop with VLLM_PORT and get_open_ports_list
vllm.author: walterbm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
When VLLM_PORT is set and the specified port is in use, _get_open_port() always starts scanning from the same base port and returns the same next-available port on every call. This causes get_open_ports_list() to loop infinitely, since it collects ports into a set that never grows beyond one

vllm.title: Change "following fields were present in the request but ignored" log from warn to debug
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Downgrade the warning if there are extra fields present in an http request so that it isn't printed by default.
At scale (1000s to 10000s of concurrent requests), a log line for each individual request can noticeably degrade performance. This means that external users of a vLLM server can degrade pe

vllm.title: [ROCm][CI] Fix tool use test stability - disable skinny GEMM, prefix caching, eliminate batch variance
vllm.author: AndreasKaratzas
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fixes intermittent tool use test failures on ROCm by applying platform-specific overrides to tests/entrypoints/openai/test_tool_calls.py.
Changes

Disable VLLM_ROCM_USE_SKINNY_GEMM (=0) via env_dict to avoid non-deterministic results from atomic reductions in the wvSplitKrc skinny GEMM kernel.
Disab

vllm.title: [Spec Decode][KV Connector] Fix KV transfer in PD + speculative decoding
vllm.author: ZhanqiuHu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Follow-up to #34529, which deferred clear_connector_metadata for speculative decoding disaggregation. This PR additionally defers wait_for_save.
CC @NickLucche
Purpose

wait_for_save() is called after the target model forward but before the drafter's forward. For NixlConnector with kv_buffer

vllm.title: [Core][KVConnector] Support HMA+NixlConnector
vllm.author: NickLucche
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Same as #32204 but with no kv blocks recovery in case of failure during xfer.
Overview
Currently connectors are not able to take full advantage of models that employ hybrid attention (FA+SWA) and treat all layers as FA, as the Hybrid Kv Cache Manager is disabled.
